qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for users to run STAR-CCM+ in graphical mode on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to use an OnDemand or JupyterLab system to initiate a remote desktop environment for running STAR-CCM+ in graphical mode.

qa_pairs.question: What environment variable needs to be set for researchers with a Power-on-Demand (PoD) license when launching STAR-CCM+ in graphical mode?
qa_pairs.answer: Researchers with a Power-on-Demand (PoD) license must set the `LM_PROJECT` environment variable to their 22-digit PoD License Key before starting `starccm+` in graphical mode, in addition to configuring the `~/.licenses/starccm.lic` file.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user launch STAR-CCM+ version 18.04.008 or newer in parallel mode on a local host through an OnDemand desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: To launch STAR-CCM+ 18.04.008 or newer in parallel mode on a local host via an OnDemand desktop, first load `StdEnv/2023`, then `starccm-mixed/20.06.010` (or `starccm/20.06.010-R8`), and finally execute `starccm+ -rr server -np 2 -mpi openmpi40` in a new terminal window.
